title: LoL Esports |
content:
Before homeguards and turret plates, there was one of the most iconic rivalries in North America: TSM vs. CLG. On July 24, we’re running this beloved matchup back in League Classic.

As part of the League Classic Takeover of the LCS Summer Split Opening Week Presented by Mastercard, we’re welcoming back some of our most iconic players (and you!) for a special showmatch featuring League of Legends’ latest release. Join us at LCS CLASSIC and re-experience the NALCS on the original Summoner’s Rift. For one day only, it’s time to pick a side again.

LCS CLASSIC: TSM vs. CLG takes place on Friday, July 24, live from the Riot Games Arena in Los Angeles.

Tickets for LCS CLASSIC go on sale at10:00 am PDT on July 15via Tixr. This is a specialty ticketed event, so make sure to secure your seat early!

Wondering which familiar faces will return to the Rift? Keep an eye on the LCSOfficial X account for roster reveals!

title: MSI 2026 Grand Final falls short of T1 vs G2 viewership peak
summary: MSI 2026 Grand Final falls short of T1 vs G2 viewership peak  Esports Charts
content:
LIVE Viewership Updates & Audience Trends

Hanwha Life EsportsdefeatedBilibili Gaming(3:2) to win the2026 Mid-Season Invitational, claiming the organization’s first MSI title in its debut appearance at the tournament. The victory also kept the trophy in South Korea for a third consecutive season, following Gen.G’s back-to-back championships in 2024 and 2025.

The grand final reached 2.2 million Peak Viewers (excluding Chinese livestreaming platforms), falling short of the 2.32 million recorded duringT1’s match againstG2 Esportsearlier in the bracket. This made MSI 2026 the first edition since 2023 in which the grand final was not the tournament’s most-watched match.

MSI 2026 recorded a noticeable year-over-year decline compared with the record-setting 2025 edition. Total Hours Watched fell by more than 17%, while Peak Viewers dropped by over 32%. Part of that decline can be explained by the tournament’s shorter airtime, with this year’s event running roughly eight hours less than in 2025.

T1’s relatively early elimination was another major factor: the team remains the biggest viewership driver not only at MSI, but across the wider League of Legends esports ecosystem, and interest naturally weakened once it exited the competition.

Average Viewers proved considerably more resilient, declining by just 7.8% year over year. This suggests that, despite the loss of its strongest audience draw and a shorter overall broadcast schedule, MSI 2026 still maintained a solid underlying level of interest throughout the event. The downturn was therefore meaningful, but less severe on a per-minute basis than the headline Hours Watched and peak figures might suggest.

title: League of Legends Classic is arriving much sooner than expected
summary: League of Legends Classic is arriving much sooner than expected  Polygon.com
content:
We'll be able to play this "greatest hits" version of League of Legends by the end of the month

Riot Games has fully unveiledLeague of Legends Classicafter teasing it a couple of weeks ago. It's coming out sooner than expected. It's being added to the game for free on July 29 as part of Patch 26.15.

These new details were revealed during the MSI 2026 Finals for LoL Esports, where anexhibition matchfor the new mode was also held. An FAQ onLeague of Legends Classic'sofficial websiteconfirms it willbe integrated right into the Riot Client players already use forLeague of Legendsand is more of a melting pot of classic features than a complete reset tothe 1.0 version of the game.

Executive Producer Paul Bellezza revealed more aboutLeague of Legends Classicduring a brief interview at the MSI Finals. He explained that the idea first emerged during a "Thunderdome" hackathon at Riot Games, when a group of developers built a prototype based onSeason 3 (2012-2013)of the game.

He also clarified that it would be a "greatest hits ofLeague" from around Season 3 rather than a completely faithful throwback. It'll launch with not just the original 40 champions but also 20 additional "classic" ones.Classicwill bring back the old way of selecting champions and runes as well as the offense, defense, and utility skills trees for the mastery system.

League of Legends Classicis part of a wave of throwback modes or re-releases of earlier versions of long-running live-service games.World of Warcraft Classicmost famously kicked off this trend, but we've also seen the likes ofFortnite OG,EverQuest Legends, andOverwatch: Classicpop up in the years since. It's fascinating that these live-service games have been running for so long that they have older versions players are nostalgic for and excited to revisit.

title: League Classic Showmatch Brings Legends Back Together

content:
Ahead of the MSI 2026 finals, Riot Games will bring fans back in time with aLeague Classicshowmatch, featuring some of the most legendary players in the game’s history.
